topic.what: Follow-up study coining the term 'vitamin U' for an anti-peptic ulcer dietary factor found in raw cabbage and other vegetables, proposing S-methylmethionine as the active compound and a mucosal integrity hypothesis for its mechanism.
topic.how: Extended case series and biochemical analysis building on the 1949 cabbage juice study; Cheney characterizes the heat-labile, water-soluble factor and tests it across a broader patient cohort; proposes that 'vitamin U' maintains gastric mucosal integrity.
topic.why_matters: Named and characterized S-methylmethionine as a discrete anti-ulcer dietary factor, establishing the mucosal integrity hypothesis that links dietary compound intake to gastric protection — a mechanistic framework relevant to dietary gastritis management.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mechanistic specificity of the proposed 'vitamin U' mucosal protection pathway
characteristics.value: Proposes that S-methylmethionine donates methyl groups supporting mucosal cell regeneration and epithelial integrity
characteristics.vs_baseline: Prior ulcer treatments focused on acid suppression rather than mucosal repair
characteristics.evidence: Combination of clinical outcome data and early biochemical characterization of the heat-labile factor
characteristics.context: Hypothesis predates modern cell biology; mechanism has been partially supported but not fully validated by subsequent research

characteristics.what_it_measures: Patient numbers supporting the vitamin U characterization
characteristics.value: Expanded cohort beyond 1949 series; multiple ulcer types (gastric, duodenal) included
characteristics.evidence: Published in American Journal of Gastroenterology 1952; exact n varies by cited version
characteristics.context: Still uncontrolled case series; no randomization; historical significance exceeds current evidentiary weight

good_for: Historical and conceptual grounding for dietary mucosal protection research
Hypothesis generation for S-methylmethionine supplementation in gastritis
Naturopathic and integrative medicine protocols emphasizing raw cruciferous vegetables
not_for: Current clinical practice guidelines for peptic ulcer or gastritis without modern validation
Replacing evidence-based H. pylori eradication or PPI therapy
requires: Modern pharmacological characterization of S-methylmethionine bioavailability and gastric mucosal mechanisms

novelty: Coined the term 'vitamin U' and identified S-methylmethionine as its likely chemical identity
Introduced the mucosal integrity hypothesis as a mechanism distinct from acid suppression for ulcer healing
open_problems: S-methylmethionine's precise mechanism of gastric mucosal protection has not been resolved at the molecular level
Whether synthetic S-methylmethionine replicates whole cabbage juice effects is unresolved
Vitamin U research largely stalled after the H. pylori discovery shifted ulcer research toward antimicrobial treatment
